text.title: Embedding Batch + Cache Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns the validator + runtime assertions catch.

antipattern.symptom: After a model swap, search quality regresses sharply but cache hit rate stays high; vectors of the wrong model/dim are returned.
antipattern.root-cause: Cache key = sha256(text) alone, no model_id/dim namespace.
antipattern.fix: Bump cache namespace. Key MUST be sha256(text)+":"+model_id+":"+dim. Validator refuses any key_scheme other than sha256-text-model-dim.

antipattern.symptom: Upsert into vector store associates the wrong vector with each ID; search returns wrong documents for matching queries.
antipattern.root-cause: Async parallel batches complete out of order; producer concatenates results in completion order, not input order.
antipattern.fix: Carry the input index with each batch; reassemble by index before return. preserve_order=true must be set in config.

antipattern.symptom: Bill is 30-60% higher than projected on workloads with high boilerplate or repeated content; identical inputs are sent to the API multiple times in one run.
antipattern.root-cause: No dedup pass before batching; same text inside one upload is paid for N times.
antipattern.fix: Build a unique-set keyed by cache key. Call provider on uniques only. Fan results back out to original positions. dedup=true in config.

antipattern.symptom: Sporadic 429 storms; partial batches lost when wrapper rethrows; total throughput LOWER than sequential.
antipattern.root-cause: concurrency set to a number unrelated to TPM/RPM; bursts exceed the per-minute window.
antipattern.fix: Set concurrency = min(rate_budget.rpm/60, rate_budget.tpm/(batch.size*avg_tokens_per_input*60)). Use a token-bucket limiter alongside asyncio.Semaphore.
